**Orem** is a city in [Utah County, Utah](/wiki/Utah_County,_Utah "Utah County, Utah"), United States, in the north central part of the state. It is adjacent to [Provo](/wiki/Provo,_Utah "Provo, Utah"), [Lindon](/wiki/Lindon,_Utah "Lindon, Utah"), and [Vineyard](/wiki/Vineyard,_Utah "Vineyard, Utah") and is approximately 45 miles (72 km) south of [Salt Lake City](/wiki/Salt_Lake_City "Salt Lake City").

Orem is one of the principal cities of the Provo-Orem, Utah [Metropolitan Statistical Area](/wiki/Provo-Orem_metropolitan_area "Provo-Orem metropolitan area"), which includes all of Utah and [Juab](/wiki/Juab_County,_Utah "Juab County, Utah") counties. The 2020 population was 98,129,[[1]](#cite_note-QuickFacts-1) while the [2010 population](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census") was 88,328[[4]](#cite_note-4) making it the [5th most populous city in Utah](/wiki/List_of_cities_and_towns_in_Utah "List of cities and towns in Utah"). [Utah Valley University](/wiki/Utah_Valley_University "Utah Valley University") is located in Orem.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

At one time the area was known as *[Sharon](/wiki/Sharon_plain "Sharon plain")*, a Biblical name for a mostly level [strip of land](/wiki/Sharon_plain "Sharon plain") running between mountains and the sea, and the name of the [Vermont birth town](/wiki/Sharon,_Vermont "Sharon, Vermont") of [Joseph Smith](/wiki/Joseph_Smith "Joseph Smith"), founder of the [Latter Day Saint movement](/wiki/Latter_Day_Saint_movement "Latter Day Saint movement").[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) Another former name was *Provo Bench.*[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)

Unlike many other early Utah communities, Orem's houses were not originally clustered in a town composed of regular city blocks. Instead, Orem's farmers dispersed their homes, building them along the territorial highway (now called State Street) and other major roads so that they could be close to both transportation corridors and to their fields and orchards.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In an apparent attempt to attract more investment to the town and provide an easy way for the large population of farmers with orchards to ship produce, in 1914 it was named after Walter C. Orem, President of the [Salt Lake and Utah Railroad](/wiki/Salt_Lake_and_Utah_Railroad "Salt Lake and Utah Railroad") in the early 1900s.[[7]](#cite_note-Rigert-7) Orem was incorporated on May 5, 1919.[[7]](#cite_note-Rigert-7)

In 2025, [Charlie Kirk](/wiki/Charlie_Kirk "Charlie Kirk"), an American [right-wing](/wiki/Right-wing_politics "Right-wing politics") activist and co-founder of [Turning Point USA](/wiki/Turning_Point_USA "Turning Point USA"), was [assassinated](/wiki/Assassination_of_Charlie_Kirk "Assassination of Charlie Kirk") at [Utah Valley University](/wiki/Utah_Valley_University "Utah Valley University").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Orem is in a high desert, with an average elevation of 4,767 ft (1,453 m). According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the city covers an area of 18.4 sq mi (48 km2), all land. Orem is near the eastern shore of [Utah Lake](/wiki/Utah_Lake "Utah Lake"), bordering [Provo](/wiki/Provo,_Utah "Provo, Utah") on the east and south, [Vineyard](/wiki/Vineyard,_Utah "Vineyard, Utah") to the west, [Lindon](/wiki/Lindon,_Utah "Lindon, Utah") contiguous to the north, and [Mount Timpanogos](/wiki/Mount_Timpanogos "Mount Timpanogos")/Wasatch Mountain range to the east.

### 2020 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2020 census")]

As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), Orem had a population of 98,129. The median age was 27.2 years. 27.2% of residents were under the age of 18 and 10.5% of residents were 65 years of age or older. For every 100 females there were 102.0 males, and for every 100 females age 18 and over there were 99.7 males age 18 and over.[[13]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-13)[[14]](#cite_note-Census2020PL-14)

99.9% of residents lived in urban areas, while 0.1% lived in rural areas.[[15]](#cite_note-Census2020DHC-15)

There were 29,920 households in Orem, of which 38.3% had children under the age of 18 living in them. Of all households, 59.3% were married-couple households, 15.8% were households with a male householder and no spouse or partner present, and 21.8% were households with a female householder and no spouse or partner present. About 16.4% of all households were made up of individuals and 6.1% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older.[[13]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-13)

There were 31,591 housing units, of which 5.3% were vacant. The homeowner vacancy rate was 0.6% and the rental vacancy rate was 6.4%.[[13]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-13)

### 2000 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2000 census")]

The 2000 Census counted 84,324 people, 23,382 households, and 19,079 families. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") at that time was 4,572.6 people per square mile (1,765.5 people/km2). There were 24,166 housing units at an average density of 1,310.4 per square mile (505.9/km2).
There were 23,382 households, out of which 48.8% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 69.0% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 9.5% had a female householder with no husband present, and 18.4% were non-families. 12.4% of all households were made up of individuals, and 5.1% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 3.57 and the average family size was 3.93.

In the city, the population was spread out, with 35.4% under the age of 18, 17.4% from 18 to 24, 25.8% from 25 to 44, 14.5% from 45 to 64, and 6.9% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 24 years. For every 100 females, there were 98.7 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 95.2 males.

The median income for a household in the city was $52,703, and the median income for a family was $59,066. Males had a median income of $42,249 versus $30,742 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the city was $20,971. About 10.3% of families and 13.8% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 16% of those under age 18 and 6% of those age 65 or over.

Nelson](/wiki/Russell_M._Nelson "Russell M. Nelson") announced a [temple](/wiki/Orem_Utah_Temple "Orem Utah Temple") in Orem on October 5, 2019.[[17]](#cite_note-17) The temple is located 0.5 miles (800 m) south of the [Interstate 15](/wiki/Interstate_15 "Interstate 15") exit at University Parkway on South Geneva Road.

## Arts and culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Arts and culture")]

### Annual cultural events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Annual cultural events")]

Oremfest is an annual, multi-day summer festival that has included different activities over the years, but most recently has featured a charity golf tournament,[[20]](#cite_note-20) pool party, outdoor concert, volunteer opportunities, car show, and parade.[[21]](#cite_note-21) Oremfest originated in 1949 to celebrate the completion of State Street.[[22]](#cite_note-22) Other events traditionally included in the festival: a baby contest, a boutique, the [Rotary Club](/wiki/Rotary_Club "Rotary Club") Breakfast, a 5K Fun Run, fireworks, a carnival, and vendor booths.

The festival came to be known as Summerfest, until Orem city officials received a [cease-and-desist](/wiki/Cease_and_desist "Cease and desist") letter in 2022 from [Milwaukee World Festival Inc.](/wiki/Milwaukee_World_Festival,_Inc. "Milwaukee World Festival, Inc."), based on that city's [Summerfest](/wiki/Summerfest "Summerfest") music festival trademark and usage of the name dating back to 1969.[[23]](#cite_note-23) In lieu of signing a licensing agreement to use the Summerfest name, the Orem city council opted instead to rebrand to Oremfest. Other Utah cities like [South Jordan](/wiki/South_Jordan,_Utah "South Jordan, Utah"), however, obtained permission use the trademarked Summerfest name for their own festivals.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

As of 2025, other city arts and cultural events[[25]](#cite_note-25) through fall and winter include:

* 9/11 Day of Service
* "Taste of Orem" (food festival)
* Harvest Festival
* Veteran's Day Program
* "Lights On" (winter light displays)

### Library

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Library")]

Orem's first library opened in 1939, before eventually relocating. The new Orem Public Library was built on the same lot as city hall, adjacent to City Center Park, under Mayor Winston Crawford's tenure in 1971.[[26]](#cite_note-26) The library features stained glass art depicting several fairy tales at a window near the children's storytelling wing, the piece was created by artist [Tom Holdman](/wiki/Tom_Holdman "Tom Holdman") at the beginning of his career.[[27]](#cite_note-27) Library Hall is an events space used for author visits, film screenings, and other programs, and includes an art gallery in the [dōTERRA](/wiki/DoTerra "DoTerra") South Lobby.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Sports")]

Orem has been home to a number of professional sports teams in addition to being the home to Utah Valley University's Wolverines athletic teams. The [Orem Owlz](/wiki/Orem_Owlz "Orem Owlz") [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_League_Baseball "Minor League Baseball") team, a [rookie league](/wiki/Rookie_League "Rookie League") affiliate of the [Los Angeles Angels](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Angels "Los Angeles Angels"), began play in 2005.[[29]](#cite_note-29) The Owlz won 5 championships and had more than 90 major league players as part of the team.[[30]](#cite_note-30) The Owlz played their home games at [UCCU Ballpark](/wiki/UCCU_Ballpark "UCCU Ballpark") on the campus of [Utah Valley University](/wiki/Utah_Valley_University "Utah Valley University"). The stadium has a capacity of 5,000 spectators.[[31]](#cite_note-31) The Owlz competed in the [Pioneer League](/wiki/Pioneer_League_(baseball) "Pioneer League (baseball)") against teams from Colorado, Idaho, Montana and one team in Utah – the [Ogden Raptors](/wiki/Ogden_Raptors "Ogden Raptors").[[32]](#cite_note-32) They relocated to [Windsor, Colorado](/wiki/Windsor,_Colorado "Windsor, Colorado"), in 2021 and became the [Northern Colorado Owlz](/wiki/Northern_Colorado_Owlz "Northern Colorado Owlz").[[33]](#cite_note-33)

Orem has also been the home to two indoor football teams as well as a G League professional basketball team. In 1998, the [Utah Catzz](/wiki/Utah_Catzz "Utah Catzz") played their only season in the [Professional Indoor Football League](/wiki/Professional_Indoor_Football_League_(1998) "Professional Indoor Football League (1998)") as the league only lasted one season. The [Utah Flash](/wiki/Utah_Flash "Utah Flash") was an [NBA G League](/wiki/NBA_G_League "NBA G League") affiliate of the [Philadelphia 76ers](/wiki/Philadelphia_76ers "Philadelphia 76ers") that was established in 2007.

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Government")]

Orem has a [council–manager government](/wiki/Council%E2%80%93manager_government "Council–manager government"). The mayor and council members are elected and serve part-time, while the city manager is appointed and serves full-time. Six city council members serve alongside the mayor. The mayor and city council are elected to staggered four-year terms.[[37]](#cite_note-37) The Orem City Center Complex on State Street housed city staffer offices from 1969 until 2025, when a new city hall was built.[[38]](#cite_note-38) The old city hall was 3D scanned before it was demolished.[[39]](#cite_note-39)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Education")]

Orem is in the [Alpine School District](/wiki/Alpine_School_District "Alpine School District")[[47]](#cite_note-47) and is home to three high schools, three junior high schools, and 14 elementary schools.[[48]](#cite_note-48) An education center of [Utah State University](/wiki/Utah_State_University "Utah State University") is also in Orem.

### Utah Valley University

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Utah Valley University")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6e/Orem_Campus_Shots_%282312918321%29.jpg/250px-Orem_Campus_Shots_%282312918321%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Orem_Campus_Shots_(2312918321).jpg)

[Utah Valley University](/wiki/Utah_Valley_University "Utah Valley University") campus

[Utah Valley University](/wiki/Utah_Valley_University "Utah Valley University") is a public university operated by the state of Utah. UVU is one of the United States' only Open Enrollment Universities, accepting all applicants. UVU offers a wide variety of bachelor's and master's degrees. It is Utah's largest and fastest-growing public university, with over 43,000 undergraduates.[[49]](#cite_note-UVU_Enrollment-49)
The campus's notable features include the [UCCU Center](/wiki/UCCU_Center "UCCU Center"), the Digital Learning Center library, the Hal Wing Track and Field Complex, and the Woodbury School of Business. The [Roots of Knowledge](/wiki/Roots_of_Knowledge "Roots of Knowledge") stained glass display is in the Fulton Library on campus.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

## Infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Infrastructure")]

### Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Transportation")]

#### Public transit

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Public transit")]

Several modes of transportation are available in Orem. The [Utah Transit Authority](/wiki/Utah_Transit_Authority "Utah Transit Authority") operates the [Frontrunner](/wiki/FrontRunner "FrontRunner") train, [Utah Valley Express](/wiki/Utah_Valley_Express "Utah Valley Express") (UVX) bus rapid transit and regular bus service in the city. The [Orem station](/wiki/Orem_(UTA_station) "Orem (UTA station)") serves Utah Transit Authority's *FrontRunner* train.[[51]](#cite_note-51) The UVX route runs from Orem Central Station through UVU and along University Parkway through Orem's uptown near its southern boundary with Provo, which is where the opposite end of the bus line is located.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

#### Major highways

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orem,_Utah&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Major highways")]

The road system includes an [Interstate highway](/wiki/Interstate_Highway_System "Interstate Highway System"), [US highways](/wiki/United_States_Numbered_Highway_System "United States Numbered Highway System"), [state highways](/wiki/State_highway "State highway"), and city-maintained roads. [Interstate 15](/wiki/Interstate_15_in_Utah "Interstate 15 in Utah") runs through the west side of Orem with four interchanges in the city.[[53]](#cite_note-53) [US Highway 89](/wiki/U.S._Route_89 "U.S. Route 89") (State Street) runs northwest–southeast through the middle of the city, while [US Highway 189](/wiki/U.S._Route_189 "U.S. Route 189") (University Avenue) passes through a short section of northeast Orem. There are also four state routes that pass through the city – [SR-52](/wiki/Utah_State_Route_52 "Utah State Route 52") (800 North/Canyon Parkway), [SR-114](/wiki/Utah_State_Route_114 "Utah State Route 114") (Geneva Road), [SR-241](/wiki/Utah_State_Route_241 "Utah State Route 241") (1600 North), and [SR-265](/wiki/Utah_State_Route_265 "Utah State Route 265") (University Parkway).[[54]](#cite_note-54)
